name: Andromeda Galaxy
info: Nearest major galaxy; on collision course with the Milky Way.

name: Sagittarius A*
info: Supermassive black hole at the Milky Way center.

name: Sirius A
info: Brightest star in Earth's night sky.

name: Proxima Centauri
info: Nearest star to the Sun.

name: TRAPPIST-1
info: Ultra-cool dwarf with seven known exoplanets.

name: Betelgeuse
info: Red supergiant in Orion; potential future supernova.

name: Vega
info: Bright A-type star in Lyra.

name: Polaris
info: Current north star; Cepheid variable.

name: Crab Nebula
info: Supernova remnant from AD 1054 event.

name: Orion Nebula
info: Stellar nursery visible to the naked eye.

name: Pleiades
info: Open star cluster in Taurus.

name: Omega Centauri
info: Massive globular cluster orbiting the Milky Way.

name: Large Magellanic Cloud
info: Irregular satellite galaxy of the Milky Way.

name: Small Magellanic Cloud
info: Dwarf galaxy companion to the Milky Way.

name: Whirlpool Galaxy
info: Classic face-on spiral galaxy (M51).

name: Sombrero Galaxy
info: Prominent edge-on spiral with dust lane.

name: Cygnus X-1
info: First widely accepted stellar-mass black hole.

name: Kepler-452b
info: Super-Earth in the habitable zone of a Sun-like star.

name: HD 209458 b
info: First exoplanet detected via transit spectroscopy.

name: Barnard's Star
info: Red dwarf with highest known proper motion.
